objective: Clarify and enforce the graphify-out artifact contract so canonical graph outputs are committed during graph creation while local cache and OS noise remain explicit exceptions.
scope_boundary: This slice is limited to hldpro-governance planning and the future governance-side artifact contract for graphify-out.
The implementation handoff may update .gitignore comments or rules, graphify governance contract tests, and documentation that defines canonical graphify artifacts.
The implementation handoff must preserve the distinction between canonical scoped graph artifacts and local-only runtime cache unless issue #241 explicitly changes cache policy.
Planner write-boundary enforcement is explicitly excluded from #241 and tracked in issue #242.
out_of_scope: Graph retrieval ranking, graph quality heuristics, Neo4j work, or model fine-tuning.
Committing graphify runtime cache files without an explicit policy decision.
Planner write-boundary enforcement, pinned-agent handoff gates, or branch/worktree policy changes.
research_summary: Repo research found that graphify-out is correctly documented and used as the canonical governance-hosted graph output root, with 46 tracked graph artifacts already present. The current noise comes from ignored runtime children inside that root: .gitignore ignores any cache directory and explicitly ignores graphify-out/cache, while .DS_Store is globally ignored. Weekly and closeout staging use broad directory staging, and the graphify contract test verifies manifest outputs live under graphify-out but does not explicitly assert which generated files are canonical or that manifest-defined outputs remain stageable. Specialty debug review recommended the smallest #241 fix: update .gitignore wording, README/COMPENDIUM artifact-contract docs, and graphify governance contract tests using git check-ignore against manifest-defined canonical artifacts and allowed local-only exceptions. Planner write-boundary enforcement is separate issue #242.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Make the graphify-out artifact contract explicit and testable.
tasks: Document which graphify-out paths are canonical tracked artifacts and which paths are local-only exceptions.
Align .gitignore wording and rules with the explicit artifact contract.
Extend graphify governance contract validation so manifest-defined output paths are not accidentally ignored and expected canonical artifacts remain stageable.
Confirm status commands distinguish canonical graph changes from ignored cache and OS files.
Update issue #241, docs/PROGRESS.md, and OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md before closeout.
Keep planner write-boundary enforcement in issue #242.
acceptance_criteria: The repo states whether graphify-out/cache is local-only or canonical.
Manifest-defined graphify output paths under graphify-out/<repo>/ are not fully ignored.
Canonical graph artifacts remain commit-ready during graph creation or refresh.
Ignored cache and .DS_Store files are not treated as completion evidence.
Planner write-boundary enforcement remains out of scope and linked to issue #242.
Any newly discovered required cleanup is completed in-scope or recorded as issue-backed follow-up before closeout.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm issue-backed planning, tracker requirements, and structured-plan requirements before creating planning artifacts.
specialist_reviews.summary: The repo requires issue-backed backlog rows, treats structured JSON as the canonical planning artifact, and allows Markdown PDCAR as companion context.

specialist_reviews.focus: Identify why graphify-out/cache appears as noise and whether graphify-out canonical artifacts are actually tracked.
specialist_reviews.summary: graphify-out/cache is ignored runtime cache inside a canonical graph root. Canonical scoped graph artifacts are tracked, while current validation does not explicitly encode the artifact allowlist/local-only split.

specialist_reviews.focus: Keep #241 scoped to graphify artifact contract and route planner write-boundary enforcement into a separate issue-backed control slice.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended keeping #241 narrow, creating #242 for the planner write-boundary gap, adding tracker rows for both, and requiring pinned-agent implementation from a fresh issue worktree.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review returned APPROVED_WITH_CHANGES: keep #241 and #242 separate, ship #241 first, and resolve #242 planner-boundary design details before implementation.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement only the #241 graphify artifact-contract fix: docs and contract-test updates that distinguish canonical graphify-out/<repo> artifacts from local-only cache/OS exceptions.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Hand #241 implementation to a pinned Tier 2 governance worker from a fresh issue worktree; do not implement planner write-boundary controls in this slice.
blocked_on: Fresh issue worktree based on current origin/main.

material_deviation_rules: Do not implement .gitignore, contract-test, or documentation changes directly from the planning role.
Do not commit graphify-out/cache unless issue #241 explicitly changes cache policy.
Do not absorb planner write-boundary enforcement into #241; use issue #242.
If implementation reveals stale legacy root graph references outside this issue's acceptance path, create an issue-backed follow-up instead of widening silently.
If any required action is found during Adjust or Review, finish it in this slice or record it as issue-backed follow-up before closeout.
